name: qollib
documentation:
# QoLlib

A Quality of Life (QoL) library that accumulates useful tools, hacks and such.

# Usage Examples
All available methods are available from the code documentation. 
Below we only highlight specific use-cases that need a bit more attention. 

Please be also invited to have a look at the unit tests ( `/tests` folder) for further code examples.

## Processing
To run code (quasi-)parallel in multiple processes or threads the `threaded` and `simultaneous` methods 
from the `processing` package remove a lot of overhead. 

Given a method that we want to execute in parallel 
```python
def long_running_calculation(num: float) -> float:
    # omitted for readability 
    pass 
```

we can simply call:
```python
simultaneous(long_running_calculation, [42, 1337, 3.141, 5, 6, 123.456, 1e-6])
```

However, common use case is that the ordering of concurrently generated results is important. 
E.g., when processing individual rows in an image. A simple trick to achieve this is to return a `tuple` from the 
processing method.

```python
import numpy as np 

def rotate_row(data: tuple) -> tuple:
    """
    | 0          | 1             | 2
    | row number | rot mat       | row data
    dat = (row: int,     rot: np.array, stokes: np.array)
    :return: (row: int, rotated_row: np.array)
    """
    rotated = np.zeros_like(data[2]).T
    for col in range(data[2].shape[1]):
        rotated[col] = np.dot(data[1], data[2][:, col])
    return data[0], rotated
```

Once our result is in this form, we can re-establish the ordering after execution by transforming the 
result into a `dict`: 

```python
args = [(row, rot, frame[:, row]) for row in range(frame.shape[1])]
res = dict(simultaneous(rotate_row, args))
rotated = np.array([res[r] for r in range(frame.shape[1])])
```

Also, when working with classes, the executing method must be accessible from outside the instance scope. 
This is best achieved with using `@staticmethod`s. 

```python
class ExampleClass:
    # ... lots of implementation details parallelize  
    @staticmethod
    def parallelize(args):
        pass

simultaneous(ExampleClass.parallelize, args)
```

## Strings
When dealing with user input it is neat to allow users to specify shapes for numpy arrays as strings in 
the common numpy format, e.g., `[13:37, :, 42]`. To extract the `tuple` of `slices` to forward this to numpy 
we provide the `parse_shape(str)` method: 

```python
data = np.arrange(10, 10)
roi = parse_shape('[1,2:4]')
print(data[roi])
#=> [12, 13]
```

modules.name: qollib.processing.thread
documentation:
The threads hold information on the function (and result) to execute in a thread or process.

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: qollib.processing.thread.Thread

methods.name: qollib.processing.thread.Thread.submit
documentation: Start execution via executor

# Thread
The threads hold information on the function to execute in a thread or process.
Provides an interface to the `future` object once submitted to an executer.

modules.name: qollib.processing.testing
documentation:
Mocks for testing multi processing functionality.

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: qollib.processing.testing.sequentially
documentation:
Method mimics the `simultaneous` or `threaded` methods, but calls everything in sequence.
\:param func: function to call
\:param args: arguments to call the function with
\:param workers: Ignored
\:return: result of the call.

modules.name: qollib.processing.execution
documentation: provides housekeeping / setup methods to reduce the programming overhead of spawning threads or processes.
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: qollib.processing.execution.threaded
documentation:
Calls the given function in multiple threads for the set of given arguments
    Note that this does not spawn processes, but threads. Use this for non CPU
    CPU dependent tasks, i.e. I/O
Method returns once all calls are done.

### Params
- func: [Function] the function to call
- args: [Iterable] the 'list' of arguments for each call
- workers: [Integer] the number of concurrent threads to use
- raise_exception: [Bool] Flag if an exception in a thread shall be raised or just logged

### Returns
Results from all `Threads` as list

functions.name: qollib.processing.execution.simultaneous
documentation:
Calls the given function in multiple processes for the set of given arguments
    Note that this does spawn processes, not threads. Use this for task that
    depend heavily on CPU and can be done in parallel.
Method returns once all calls are done.

### Params
- func: [Function] the function to call
- args: [Iterable] the 'list' of arguments for each call
- workers: [Integer] the number of concurrent threads to use (Default: NUM_CPUs)
- raise_exception: [Bool] Flag if an exception in a thread shall be raised or just logged

### Returns
Results from all `Threads` as list

functions.name: qollib.processing.execution._collect_results
documentation:
Takes a list of threads and busy waits for them to be executed.

### Params
- threads: [List<Thread>] a list of submitted threads
- raise_exception: [Bool] Flag if an exception in a thread shall be raised or just logged

### Returns
Results from all `Threads` as list

variables.name: qollib.processing.execution.CPU_LIM
documentation: 70% of CPUs from the current machine (max usage limit)

modules.name: qollib.collections.chunking
documentation:
The `chunking` utility provides methods to break huge collections in chunks

@author: hoelken
!/usr/bin/env python3
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: qollib.collections.chunking.chunker
documentation:
Generates chunks (slices) from a given sequence

### Params
- seq: the list to chunk
- size: the size of the chunks

### Returns
A list of lists where each list has the
    length of the requested chunk size (maybe except the last one)

functions.name: qollib.collections.chunking.indexed_chunks
documentation:
Generates indexed chunks (slices) from a given sequence
### Params
- seq: List the list to chunk
- size: Integer the size of the chunks

### Returns
 A dictionary with the index as key and the corresponding chunk as value.
    The length of the value arrays is the requested chunk size (maybe except the last one)

modules.name: qollib.collections.reducing

documentation: Print a progress dot to std. out

functions.name: qollib.strings.np_shape_parser.parse_shape
documentation:
Parses a numpy like shape string, e.g. [0:,42:1337]
:returns: a tuple of slices that can be used with numpy arrays

functions.name: qollib.strings.np_shape_parser.to_slice
documentation: :returns: a slice with start and stop taken from the string, seperated by delimiter, e.g. 3:7

functions.name: qollib.strings.np_shape_parser.to_int
documentation: :returns: an integer if the string is numeric

functions.name: qollib.strings.numeric.sort_num
documentation:
Applies a sorting algorithm to a list of strings containing an integer number.
Selecting the first integer of the string and sort by it (regardless of zero-padded or not)

functions.name: qollib.strings.numeric.extract_int
documentation:
Extracts an integer from a string with a number.
If multiple numbers, seperated by nun number characters, exist, the first one will be extracted.
